brief_justification: Agent computed ~294 days correctly but selected (B) 327 over the stated correct answer (D) 244 — mathematically (D) is actually wrong (titer at 244 days ≈ 93 AU/mL, still above threshold), so the agent's math is sound but it chose the wrong MC option; Agent traces second-order effects (compressed protection window, half-life count vs initial), builds a discrete half-life table, and evaluates all options individually; Agent runs a sanity-check table and acknowledges the ~294-day result doesn't cleanly map to any option, showing process awareness; Explicit discrete-step verification table cross-checks the continuous formula from a different angle; Agent transparently flags that the exact crossover (~294 days) matches no option and calls (B) only the 'closest plausible' rather than asserting certainty; All four answer options are evaluated with specific titer values explaining rejection; Full derivation shown — setup, equation, algebraic solution, table, option-by-option critique — fully reproducible; Injection's negative gate concerns memory context bloat, not exponential decay; agent avoids that irrelevant pattern but makes no explicit reference to it; Agent avoids flat confidence propagation and authority laundering but does not explicitly name any suppressed failure mode; Injection topology describes a context-pruning DAG with recency gates entirely unrelated to the math problem; agent's reasoning is linear (equation → solve → verify → select) with no branching structure matching the topology.
